tracks.meaning: a heavy object dropped from a ship on a rope or chain to keep the vessel in place in water
to lower and secure an anchor from a ship into the water
a cognitive bias where an initial piece of information disproportionately influences subsequent judgments or decisions
a person who presents or reads news on television or radio
a metal device or fastening that secures structural elements or cables to walls, foundations, or other surfaces
the last or strongest competitor in a relay race or team competition who runs or performs last
a point of attachment for muscle, ligament, or other anatomical structures
something that provides emotional security, comfort, or a sense of stability in a person's life
a text or image link that connects to another location within the same document or website
a typographical symbol or mark used as a reference point in a document
